## Top 5

**1. [019-experiment.txt](experiments/019-experiment.txt)**  
Open-world models need preconceptual novelty signals, but drifting replay and hollow labels corrupt memory, making reality mistaken for well-labeled fiction.

**2. [006-experiment.txt](experiments/006-experiment.txt)**  
Parameter isolation creates module graveyards; stable outputs in unvisited regions may be stranded function, so continual learners must preserve principled uncertainty.

**3. [013-experiment.txt](experiments/013-experiment.txt)**  
Continual learning needs multidimensional, auditable memory under concept and ontology drift; critique: novelty is moderate, but claims are partly testable.

**4. [018-experiment.txt](experiments/018-experiment.txt)**  
Benchmarks can mask drift; meta-learning can overfit change patterns; robust continual learners must preserve unresolved uncertainty, not just retain knowledge.

**5. [020-experiment.txt](experiments/020-experiment.txt)**  
Benchmarks with explicit task boundaries can mask deployment risks: hidden shifts may raise average accuracy while worsening interpretability, editability, and rare-case failures.
